LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JANET KASSOUF'S MOTION TO ENFORCE STIPULATION OF SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. THE STIPULATION FOR SETTLEMENT IS A BINDING SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND ITS TERMS ARE SUBJECT TO ENFORCEMENT BY THIS COURT PER CCP 664.6. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE PHRASE "STANDARD FORM OF A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND RELEASE" IN PARAGRAPH 3 OF THE STIPULATION DOES NOT CONTEMPLATE THE INCLUSION OF ANY TERMS IN THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT OTHER THAN ALREADY SPECIFIED IN PARAGRAPHS 1-2 AND 4-6 OF THE STIPULATION OTHER THAN THAT THE RELEASE LANGUAGE WOULD BE MUTUAL RATHER THAN MERELY ONE-SIDED. PARAGRAPH 3 CONTEMPLATED A MUTUAL RELEASE, NOTWITHSTANDING THE ONE-SIDED RELEASE IN PARAGRAPH 2, BECAUSE BOTH SIDES ASSERTED CLAIMS AGAINST EACH OTHER. THE COURT REJECTS THE POSITION OF DEFENDANTS ALLEN AND JANETTE BLAZICK THAT THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT INCLUDED A CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT, A LIQUIDATED DAMAGES CLAUSE, LANGUAGE REGARDING TAX OBLIGATIONS OR ANY OTHER TERMS SOUGHT BY THE BLAZICKS AFTER THEY SIGNED THE STIPULATION THAT ARE NOT EXPRESSLY STATED IN PARAGRAPHS 1-2 AND 4-6 OF THE STIPULATION. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189

REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS ALLEN AND JANETTE BLAZICKS DEMURRER TO THE VERIFIED "AMENDED COMPLAINT" OF PLAINTIFF JANET KASSOUF (TRANSACTION ID # 58246113) FILED BY DEFENDANT BLAZICK, ALLEN BLAZICK, JANETTE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALLEN BLAZICK AND JANETTE BLAZICK'S DEMURRER TO THE VERIFIED AMENDED COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF JANET KASSOUF. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT CONFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE BLAZICKS' DEMURRER TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED AGAINST THEM IS OVERRULED. THE COURT COULD NOT LOCATE A COMPLAINT FILED ON JUNE 12, 2015 AND TREATED THE BLAZICKS' DEMURRER AS ATTACKING THE CLAIMS ALLEGED IN THE "VERIFIED AMENDED COMPLAINT" FILED ON JUNE 10, 2015. WHILE PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE SOUGHT LEAVE TO FILE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO HER NEW CLAIMS AND ALLEGATIONS AGAINST THE BLAZICKS, HER FAILURE TO DO SO HAS NOT PREJUDICED THE BLAZICKS AND THUS THE COURT WILL TREAT THE AMENDED COMPLAINT AS HAVING BEEN PROPERLY FILED AGAINST THE BLAZICKS. THE BLAZICKS' REQUEST TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE CONTENTS OF THE SHAREHOLDER AGREEMENT IS DENIED. THE CONTENTS OF A CONTRACT ARE NOT A PROPER SUBJECT FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. THE EXCULPATORY CLAUSE IN THE CERTIFICATE OF INCORPORATION DOES NOT NECESSARILY BAR ANY OF THE CLAIMS ALLEGED AGAINST THE BLAZICKS. NONE OF THE CLAIMS ALLEGED AGAINST THE BLAZICKS ARE CLEARLY TIME-BARRED FROM THE FACE OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST THE BLAZICKS ARE NOT SO VAGUE AS TO BE SUBJECT TO A DEMURRER AND ALL OTHER CHALLENGES TO PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS MADE BY THE BLAZICKS LACK MERIT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulation Of Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-23-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JANET KASSOUF's Motion To Enforce Stipulation Of Settlement is granted. The Stipulation for Settlement is a binding settlement agreement and its terms are subject to enforcement by this court per CCP 664.6. The court finds that the phrase "standard form of a Settlement Agreement and Release" in paragraph 3 of the Stipulation does not contemplate the inclusion of any terms in the parties' agreement other than already specified in paragraphs 1-2 and 4-6 of the Stipulation other than that the release language would be mutual rather than merely one-sided. Paragraph 3 contemplated a mutual release, notwithstanding the one-sided release in paragraph 2, because both sides asserted claims against each other. The court rejects the position of defendants Allen and Janette Blazick that the parties' settlement included a confidentiality agreement, a liquidated damages clause, language regarding tax obligations or any other terms sought by the Blazicks after they signed the Stipulation that are not expressly stated in paragraphs 1-2 and 4-6 of the Stipulation. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Off Calendar Sep-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JANET KASSOUF'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum is GRANTED and $4,000 in sanctions are AWARDED. Court adopts the judge pro tem's recommendation. A party who seeks discovery of constitutionally protected information such as the at-issue bank records must show that it is "directly relevant" to a claim or defense, not just relevant to the subject matter. (Harris v. Superior Court (1992) 3 Cal.App.4th 661, 665.) Defendant Blazick abjectly failed to make that showing here. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To The Verified Amended Complaint Of Plaintiff Janet Kassouf
Off Calendar Dec-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALLEN BLAZICK and JANETTE BLAZICK'S Demurrer To The Verified Amended Complaint Of Plaintiff Janet Kassouf - The Blazicks' demurrer to all causes of action alleged against them is overruled. The court could not locate a complaint filed on June 12, 2015 and treated the Blazicks' demurrer as attacking the claims alleged in the "verified amended complaint" filed on June 10, 2015. While plaintiff should have sought leave to file the amended complaint as to her new claims and allegations against the Blazicks, her failure to do so has not prejudiced the Blazicks and thus the court will treat the amended complaint as having been properly filed against the Blazicks. The Blazicks' request to take judicial notice of the contents of the shareholder agreement is denied. The contents of a contract are not a proper subject for judicial notice. The exculpatory clause in the certificate of incorporation does not necessarily bar any of the claims alleged against the Blazicks. None of the claims alleged against the Blazicks are clearly time-barred from the face of the amended complaint. Plaintiff's claims against the Blazicks are not so vague as to be subject to a demurrer and all other challenges to plaintiffs' claims made by the Blazicks lack merit. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

Off Calendar Jun-22-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard Weaver, in pro per, for defendant;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT JANET KASSOUF'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend against Plaintiff Kassouf. Cross Complainants cite no authority under California law allowing them to allege equitable indemnity against Plaintiff. The doctrine of equitable indemnity applies among defendants who are jointly and severally liable to the plaintiff. [See GEM Developers v. Hallcraft Homes of San Diego, Inc.
